authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(15) — Big Lagoon (Humboldt Co.)
sources.quotedText: (15) Big Lagoon (Humboldt Co.). For purposes of this subsection, the boundary between Big Lagoon and Maple Creek is the first private road bridge, located approximately 1/2 mile southeast of the Highway 101 bridge crossing. All year. Only barbless hooks may be used. Cutthroat Trout minimum size limit: 10 inches. 2 Cutthroat Trout. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (CDFW booklet)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40 — Hatchery Trout/Steelhead Footnote (**) Definition
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: USGS GNIS gaz_id 219209, "Big Lagoon", Lake, Humboldt County, CA — coordinate cross-check (HomeTownLocator/Wikipedia)
sources.quotedText: GNIS Feature ID 219209, feature class Lake, Humboldt County, California. Wikipedia's infobox for the Big Lagoon water body (sourced from GNIS) lists coordinates 41°11′00″N 124°07′00″W (41.18333, -124.11667), matching the file's recorded coordinate (-124.1164, 41.1819) to within ~160m. Note: gaz_id 219209 is absent from the USGS geonames ArcGIS MapServer/7 mirror (neighboring gaz_ids 219207 and 219208, other 'Big Lagoon' features in San Mateo and Marin counties, do resolve there), so this third-party/Wikipedia cross-check was used as the fallback per dataset convention.

parameters.note: Only barbless hooks may be used; bait and lures are permitted as long as the hook is barbless.

regulations.humanSummary: Only barbless hooks may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Cutthroat Trout minimum size limit: 10 inches
regulations.verbatimText: Cutthroat Trout minimum size limit: 10 inches.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 Cutthroat Trout daily bag and possession limit, separate from the hatchery trout/steelhead limit below.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 Cutthroat Trout daily bag and possession limit

parameters.counting_rule: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily; 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead in possession. Limit applies to hatchery (adipose fin-clipped) fish only, separate from the Cutthroat Trout limit above.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.condition: Trout or steelhead lacking a healed adipose fin clip (i.e. wild/unmarked fish)
parameters.note: Only hatchery-marked fish (healed adipose fin clip) may be retained; all other trout and steelhead must be released immediately unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery trout/steelhead may be kept; unmarked wild fish must be released immediately.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
